message:
refactor(fcp-core/capability): allow borrowed tokens in CapabilityVerifier + drop phantom S: Clone bound

Before this change three ergonomic sharp edges piled up at every
verify*() call site:

1. The derived Clone on CapabilityToken<S> inherited an S: Clone
   bound from the phantom marker types (Unverified, CryptographicallyVerified,
   BoundVerified, UnboundVerified). Those marker structs don't and
   shouldn't implement Clone, so .clone() on a token worked only
   because each state currently happens to be a unit struct that
   auto-derives Clone — one day it won't, and the breakage is
   spooky-action-at-a-distance.

2. CapabilityVerifier::verify / verify_bound / verify_unbound all took
   an owned CapabilityToken. Callers holding &CapabilityToken in a
   request struct (every connector adapter invoke() path) had to
   .clone() the token just to pass it through verification, allocating
   a fresh COSE_Sign1 payload per call for no reason.

3. There is no From<&CapabilityToken<S>> for CapabilityToken<S>, so
   callers cannot uniformly say `verifier.verify(&token, ...)` with a
   borrowed token today.

Fix:

- Replace the derived Clone with a manual Clone impl that does NOT
  require S: Clone — it clones the owned fields (raw CoseToken,
  verified_claims) and keeps the PhantomData zero-sized. Phantom state
  marker types can now be non-Clone without affecting token cloning.
- Add From<&CapabilityToken<S>> for CapabilityToken<S> so borrowed
  tokens convert via the same Into bound as owned tokens.
- Genericize verify / verify_bound / verify_unbound over T: Into<CapabilityToken>.
  Both &CapabilityToken and CapabilityToken now flow through verify
  without clone-at-call-site. The first line of each function is
  `let token = token.into();` so the implementation stays unchanged
  beyond that and the deprecation / instance_id guards remain in
  their original order.

Propagate to the two e2e adapters that already held req.capability_token
by reference in their invoke path (DocuSign, LLM Router): drop the
implicit clone by passing `&req.capability_token` directly. Airtable /
Google Docs / Google Sheets / S3 adapters will pick this up in a
separate pass (their invoke() path additionally moves them behind a
Mutex; see the adjacent commit).

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
